title: Past Tense
description: Talk about what happened — with the correct feminine endings that mark you as fluent.
culturalNote: Hindi past tense agrees with gender AND sometimes with the object, not just the subject. As a woman, you'll often say 'gayi' where a man says 'gaya'. Getting this right is a huge marker of fluency — Delhi people notice immediately.

exercises.note: Feminine past of 'jaana' (to go). 'Main kal market gayi' = I went to the market yesterday. Masculine: 'gaya'. 'Woh kahan gayi?' = where did she go? Don't say 'gaya' — that's the masculine form.
Feminine past of 'aana' (to come). 'Main kal aayi' = I came yesterday. 'Woh aayi' = she came. Masculine: 'aaya'. 'Kab aayi tum?' = when did you come? Very common — 'aayi kya?' = did you arrive?

'Kiya' = did. 'Maine kiya' = I did it. 'Tune kya kiya?' = what did you do? For transitive verbs, past tense agrees with the object, not subject — so women also say 'kiya' (not 'kiyi'). This is a key grammar rule.

exercises.prompt: You went to a party last night. How do you tell your friend?
distractors: Main kal party jaa rahi thi

exercises.note: Feminine past of 'hona' (to be). 'Main wahan thi' = I was there. 'Woh bohot khush thi' = she was very happy. Masculine: 'tha'. 'Kahan thi tum?' = where were you? Use for describing past states.

'Maine khaya' = I ate. 'Tune kya khaya?' = what did you eat? Agrees with food (object), not eater (subject) — so 'maine pizza khaya' (pizza is masculine) but 'maine roti khayi' (roti is feminine). Start with just 'khaya' for now.
Feminine past of 'sona' (to sleep). 'Main raat ko der se soyi' = I slept late last night. 'Kab soyi thi?' = when did you go to sleep? Masculine: 'soya'. 'Der se soyi kyun?' = why did you sleep late?

exercises.sentence: Kal main bohot thak _____, isliye jaldi so gayi.
exercises.sentenceEnglish: Yesterday I was very tired, so I went to sleep early.

exercises.note: 'Maine dekha' = I saw. 'Tune woh movie dekhi?' = did you watch that movie? (Here 'movie' is feminine so it becomes 'dekhi'). In casual speech, people often just say 'dekha' — it's understood. 'Dekha nahi' = I didn't see.

'Maine usse baat ki' = I talked to him/her. 'Tune mujhse baat kyun nahi ki?' = why didn't you talk to me? 'Baat karna' = to talk. 'Ki' is feminine (baat is feminine) — so this stays 'ki' regardless of who spoke.

exercises.prompt: You want to say 'She was not at home.' Which is correct?
distractors: Woh ghar par nahi thi hoon
Woh ghar par nahi hai thi

exercises.instruction: Match the verb to its feminine past form

exercises.sentence: Maine kal usse call _____ lekin usne nahi uthaya.
exercises.sentenceEnglish: I called her yesterday but she didn't pick up.
exercises.hint: 'Kiya' = did (transitive verbs use kiya regardless of speaker's gender)

exercises.prompt: Which sentence means 'I went to the office and came back late'?
exercises.correctAnswer: Main office gayi aur der se aayi
distractors: Main office gaya aur der se aayi
Main office gayi aur der se aaya
Main office gayi aur der se aaye
exercises.hint: Both verbs need feminine forms: gayi and aayi
